subject: China Northern Rare Earth (Group) High-Tech; JL MAG Rare-Earth; Yunnan Chihong Zinc & Germanium
sought: any sourced relationship edge for these three flagged chokepoints
searched: their own Shanghai and Shenzhen annual reports (used to cost them, FY2025); the counterparty-side filings that produced this map's other edges
found_instead: all three are costed at tier 1 from their own filings, but neither those filings nor any Western counterparty document names a trading partner this map carries.
blocks: three of this map's five chokepoints sit on a map labelled Mapped with no sourced edge at all, so the central claim of the map is unevidenced for a majority of its chokepoints
would_close_it: a counterparty-side filing naming them (the route that worked for Shenghe via MP Materials and Tianqi via SQM), or a customs and trade dataset naming shipper and consignee

subject: Lynas Rare Earths
sought: a commercial customer for Lynas separated rare earth product
searched: Lynas Rare Earths annual report, full text
found_instead: the report names the US Department of Defense and no commercial customer at all.
blocks: the largest rare earth separator outside China connects to this map only through a government counterparty
would_close_it: a Lynas offtake announcement naming a buyer, or a magnet maker naming Lynas as a supplier in its own filing

subject: Glencore plc -> Umicore
sought: current evidence for Glencore supplying cobalt to Umicore
searched: the 2019-05-29 Glencore press release cited on the edge
found_instead: the release is genuine and tier 2, but Glencore idled the Mutanda mine shortly after it was published, so the arrangement it describes may no longer hold.
blocks: this map's oldest edge by seven years, and it was flagged as needing a refresh in the burst that created it
would_close_it: a current Glencore or Umicore annual report restating the supply arrangement

subject: Syrah Resources
searched: Syrah Resources FY2025 annual report, full text, with a positive control (Tesla, Lucid, Balama return hits) and a negative control (Volkswagen returns none)
found_instead: Syrah's real offtakes run to Tesla, Ford, LG Energy Solution and POSCO. Tesla is on this map; the others are not, so the edges cannot be drawn without expanding the roster.
blocks: an anode-layer chokepoint candidate looks unconnected because of this map's company list, not because the relationships are absent
would_close_it: adding the missing battery and automaker nodes to companies.csv, the same scope fix that unblocked this map's other offtake edges

subject: MP Materials -> Lynas Rare Earths
sought: current evidence for the MP Materials and Lynas relationship as recorded on this edge
searched: the 2023-02-28 source cited on the edge
found_instead: the source is genuine but three years older than anything else on this map, and both companies have since announced major capacity and policy changes.
blocks: nothing on its own, but it is the second oldest edge here and the oldest one is already flagged
would_close_it: either company's most recent annual report restating the relationship

subject: POSCO Future M
sought: current confirmation of the Syrah and POSCO offtake
searched: Syrah Resources FY2025 annual report, full text; FY2024 (the row's source); FY2023
found_instead: FY2025 DOES NOT MENTION POSCO AT ALL. FY2023 said the offtake required POSCO's notification by 31 Dec 2025 to commence. The row is tier 1 but dated FY2024 and confidence=medium because the worker refused to date it to a document that does not support it.
blocks: the offtake may simply have lapsed, and the edge as drawn conveys a live arrangement
would_close_it: POSCO Future M's or Syrah's next annual report either restating the offtake or confirming it did not commence

subject: Syrah Resources
sought: whether Syrah's Ford, LG Energy Solution and Samsung SDI arrangements ever became binding
searched: Syrah Resources FY2023, FY2024 and FY2025 annual reports, full text
found_instead: FY2023 and FY2024 carry only NON-BINDING MoUs 'to evaluate AAM supply ... and progress towards binding offtake agreements'. All three VANISH ENTIRELY from FY2025, suggesting they never converted. No relationship_type in this repo fits 'agreement to evaluate', and writing offtake-with would have been an overclaim.
blocks: three counterparties that a reader would expect on an anode-layer map are absent, and the reason is not visible anywhere on the map
would_close_it: a later Syrah filing either announcing binding offtakes or confirming the MoUs lapsed; separately, a vocabulary term for a non-binding intent would let the state be drawn at all

subject: Syrah Resources -> Tesla Inc
sought: whether the Syrah and Tesla offtake is still in force
searched: Syrah Resources FY2025 annual report, full text, which is the edge's own cited source
found_instead: the SAME filing that evidences this offtake also discloses that Tesla alleged Syrah defaulted on conforming AAM samples, with a cure deadline of 1 JUNE 2026 after which Tesla may terminate. That deadline has now passed. The edge renders as a plain offtake and conveys none of this.
blocks: the map's most recognisable offtake edge may already be terminated, and its own evidence says so
would_close_it: any Syrah or Tesla disclosure after 1 June 2026 stating whether the default was cured or the agreement terminated
